questions.text: IUPAC name of C6H5 - CH2 - CH2 - NH2 is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The parent hydrocarbon is ethane. The substituent (phenyl group) is present at a second carbon atom.Hence, the IUPAC name is 2-phenyl ethanamine.

2-phenyl ethanamide, also known as phenylacetamide, is an organic compound with the chemical formula C8H9NO. It is classified as an amide and consists of a phenyl group (C6H5) attached to the amino group (NH2) of an ethanamide (CH3CONH) molecule.Phenylacetamide is a white crystalline solid that is sparingly soluble in water. It is primarily used as an intermediate in the synthesis of various pharmaceuticals, agrochemicals, and other organic compounds. It can be chemically modified to produce a wide range of derivatives with different properties and applications.
The parent hydrocarbon is ethane. The substituent (phenyl group) is present at a second carbon atom. Hence, the IUPAC name is 2-phenyl ethanamine.

3-phenyl ethanamine: This name is incorrect because "3-phenyl" indicates the presence of a phenyl group attached to the third carbon atom of the parent chain. However, in the given compound, the phenyl group is attached to the first carbon atom.

questions.explanation: The compound C6H5−CH2−CH2−NH2 is an amine. The parent hydrocarbon is ethane. The substituent (phenyl group) is present at a second carbon atom.Hence, the IUPAC name is 2-phenylethanamine.

questions.text: In alkanes, each carbon has hybridization:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The carbon atoms in alkanes are sp3 hybridized, and have tetrahedral shapes, with the bonded atoms at angles of 109.5° to each other. Free rotation is possible around the carbon-carbon single bonds in alkanes, making the carbon chains very flexible and "floppy" — particularly for the larger molecules.

questions.text: Which of the following has the lowest vapour pressure at 20 degrees Centigrade?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Diethyl ether has a lower boiling point and weaker intermolecular forces compared to both water and chloroform. It is a volatile compound that readily evaporates, resulting in a higher vapor pressure than water and chloroform at 20 degrees Celsius.

Chloroform has weaker intermolecular forces than water. It is a polar molecule with a partial negative charge on the chlorine atom and partial positive charges on the hydrogen atoms. These dipole-dipole interactions result in a higher vapor pressure compared to water but lower than diethyl ether and carbon tetrachloride.

Carbon tetrachloride is a nonpolar molecule and has only weak dispersion forces between its molecules. It has the weakest intermolecular forces among the given substances, which allows its molecules to escape more easily and form a vapor. Thus, carbon tetrachloride has the highest vapor pressure at 20 degrees Celsius compared to water, chloroform, and diethyl ether.Carbon tetrachloride is a nonpolar molecule and has only weak dispersion forces between its molecules. It has the weakest intermolecular forces among the given substances, which allows its molecules to escape more easily and form a vapor. Thus, carbon tetrachloride has the highest vapor pressure at 20 degrees Celsius compared to water, chloroform, and diethyl ether.

Water has hydrogen bonding between its molecules, which leads to strong intermolecular forces. These forces hold the water molecules together tightly, making it more difficult for them to escape and form a vapor. Therefore, water has a relatively low vapor pressure at 20 degrees Celsius compared to the other substances.

questions.explanation: The boiling point of a substance is a measure of the strength of the intermolecular forces between the molecules of the substance. The stronger the intermolecular forces, the higher the boiling point. Water has strong hydrogen bonding interactions between the molecules, which is why it has the highest boiling point.The vapor pressure of a substance is the pressure exerted by the vapor of the substance above the liquid. The lower the vapor pressure, the less likely the molecules of the substance are to evaporate. Water has a low vapor pressure at 20 degrees Centigrade because the strong intermolecular forces make it difficult for the molecules to escape from the liquid.The other three substances, diethyl ether, chloroform, and carbon tetrachloride, have weaker intermolecular forces than water. This is why they have lower boiling points and higher vapor pressures at 20 degrees Centigrade.Therefore, water has the lowest vapor pressure at 20 degrees Celsius among the substances provided.

questions.text: Isomerism due to shifting of proton from one atom to another in a same molecule is known as:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Metamerism is the type of isomerism in which chemical compounds have identical proportions of the same elements and the same molecular weight, but have radicals differing in type or position, with resulting differences in chemical properties.

Tautomerism is the existence of two or more chemical compounds that are capable of facile inter-conversion, in many cases merely exchanging a hydrogen atom between two other atoms, to either of which it forms a covalent bond.

Positional isomers are constitutional isomers with the same carbon skeleton and will have the same functional group. They differ from each other in the position of the functional groups on the carbon chain and the phenomenon is known as positional isomerism.

Functional Isomerism is also known as functional group isomerism. As the name suggests, it refers to the compounds that have the same chemical formula but different functional groups attached to them.

questions.explanation: A tautomer of a compound refers to the isomer of the compound which only differs in the position of protons and electrons so, B is correct.In the rest of the options (A, C, and D), there is a difference in the positioning of whole atoms and not just protons, which is why these options are incorrect.

questions.text: Which one of them is used as an automobile antifreeze:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: False.
False.

Ethylene glycol (antifreeze) is used in the cooling of a car radiator during the winter because it has a much lower freezing point than water. The specific heat of antifreeze (pure or mixed with water) is lower than that of pure water. But in the summer water in a car radiator serves as a coolant.
False.

questions.explanation: Ethylene glycol (antifreeze) is used in the cooling of a car radiator during the winter because it has a much lower freezing point than water. The specific heat of antifreeze (pure or mixed with water) is lower than that of pure water. But in the summer water in a car radiator serves as a coolant.

questions.text: Which one of them is used as an automobile antifreeze?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Ethanol is not used as automobile antifreeze. Ethanol is a type of alcohol that is commonly used as a fuel additive or solvent.
Ethene is not used as an automobile antifreeze because it is a gas at room temperature and pressure and does not have the necessary properties for use as an antifreeze.

Ethylene glycol (antifreeze) is used in the cooling of a car radiator during the winter because it has a much lower freezing point than water. Its role in an automobile is to absorb heat from the engine
Propene is not used as automobile antifreeze. Propene is a hydrocarbon that is used as a feedstock for the production of plastics and other chemicals.

questions.explanation: Option A: Ethanol is not used as automobile antifreeze. Ethanol is a type of alcohol that is commonly used as a fuel additive or solvent. Option B: Ethene is not used as an automobile antifreeze because it is a gas at room temperature and pressure and does not have the necessary properties for use as an antifreeze. Option C: Ethylene glycol (antifreeze) is used in the cooling of a car radiator during the winter because it has a much lower freezing point than water. Its role in an automobile is to absorb heat from the engine. Option D: Propene is not used as automobile antifreeze. Propene is a hydrocarbon that is used as a feedstock for the production of plastics and other chemicals.

questions.text: When are the prefixes ortho, meta, and para used?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: In alkynes to identify the position of the triple bond
options.explanation: They are not used to identify the position of the triple bond.

options.text: In arenes to identify the position of the first substitution
options.explanation: They are not used to identify the position of the first substitution.

options.text: In arenes to identify the position of the second substitution
options.explanation: The terms ortho, meta, and para are prefixes used in organic chemistry to indicate the position of non-hydrogen substituents on a hydrocarbon ring (benzene derivative). The prefixes derive from Greek words meaning correct/straight, following/after, and similar, respectively.

options.text: In all hydrocarbons to identify the position of the functional group
options.explanation: They are not used to identify the position of the functional group.

questions.explanation: The terms ortho, meta, and para are prefixes used in organic chemistry to indicate the position of non-hydrogen substituents on a hydrocarbon ring (benzene derivative). The prefixes derive from Greek words meaning correct/straight, following/after, and similar, respectively.

questions.text: Friedrich prepared UREA from:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Friedrich Wöhler prepared urea from ammonium cyanate in 1828. This was a groundbreaking discovery as it was the first time an organic compound, urea, was successfully synthesized from inorganic starting materials. Before this, it was believed that organic compounds could only be obtained from living organisms through vitalism.The reaction Wöhler used to synthesize urea from ammonium cyanate is known as the Wöhler synthesis: NH4CNO + heat → (NH2)2CO + NH3In this reaction, ammonium cyanate ((NH4)2CO) is heated, and it rearranges to form urea ((NH2)2CO) and ammonia (NH3). This marked a significant turning point in the field of organic chemistry and helped disprove the theory of vitalism. It showed that organic compounds could be synthesized from inorganic sources, contributing to the foundation of modern organic chemistry.

This marked a significant turning point in the field of organic chemistry and helped disprove the theory of vitalism. It showed that organic compounds could be synthesized from inorganic sources, contributing to the foundation of modern organic chemistry.

This marked a significant turning point in the field of organic chemistry and helped disprove the theory of vitalism. It showed that organic compounds could be synthesized from inorganic sources, contributing to the foundation of modern organic chemistry.

This marked a significant turning point in the field of organic chemistry and helped disprove the theory of vitalism. It showed that organic compounds could be synthesized from inorganic sources, contributing to the foundation of modern organic chemistry.

questions.explanation: The 'Wöhler synthesis' is the conversion of ammonium cyanate into urea. This chemical reaction was described in 1828 by Friedrich Wöhler.

questions.text: SN1 reactions are favored by which of the following reactions?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Water is a polar solvent; hence, this option is the correct one.
Benzene is nonpolar; hence, the option is incorrect.
Carbon tetrachloride is nonpolar; hence, the option is incorrect.
Carbon disulfide is nonpolar; hence, the option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: SN1 reactions are favored by Polar prototic Solvents. These are polar solvents that are able to show hydrogen bonding.

questions.text: During the course of a SN1 reaction, the intermediate species formed is:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: As the carbon atom becomes positively charged, it is called a carbocation. The option is the correct one.
The carbon atom does not become a free radical because there is no homolytic breakage. Hence, this option is incorrect
After the breakage, the carbon atom does not have a negative charge on it. So it does not become a carbanion. Hence, this option is incorrect.
Intermediate complexes are formed in coordination reactions, which are not related to SN reactions. Hence, this option is incorrect

questions.explanation: In the first step of an SN1 reaction, the halide atom of the alkyl halide is removed, and the carbon atom becomes sp2 hybridized. The halide ion is highly electronegative, and there is heterolytic bond breakage. It results in a positive charge on the C-atom and it becomes a carbocation.

questions.text: Which one of the following is an electrophile?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Br- has a negative charge, and it donates its positive charge. So, it is a nucleophile, and this option is incorrect.
CH4 is neither a nucleophile nor an electrophile. However, its radical, -CH3+ has electrophilic properties. So, out of all options, this option is the most suitable one. So this option is correct.
NH3 has a lone pair that acts as a nucleophile. Hence, this option is also incorrect.
H2O has 2 lone pairs, and any one of them acts as a nucleophile. Hence, this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: An electrophile is a species that lack electrons or has a positive charge on it. Br- is a nucleophile, along with H2O and NH3. CH4 is neither a nucleophile nor an electrophile. However, its radical, -CH+3 has electrophilic properties. So, out of all options, B option is the most suitable one.

questions.text: Wohler prepared urea in the laboratory for the first time in _ ?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This option is not correct.Wöhler prepared urea in the laboratory for the first time in 1828.
This option is not correct.Wöhler prepared urea in the laboratory for the first time in 1828.
This option is not correct.Wöhler prepared urea in the laboratory for the first time in 1828.

Wöhler prepared urea in the laboratory for the first time in 1828. Friedrich Wöhler, a German chemist, synthesized urea from ammonium cyanate, marking a historic moment in science as it was the first organic compound synthesized from an inorganic source.This discovery disproved the "vital force theory", which claimed that living organisms could only produce organic compounds.

questions.explanation: In 1828, Wohler synthesized urea by slowly evaporating a water solution of ammonium cyanate, which he had prepared by adding silver cyanate to ammonium chloride. So, option D is accurate.

questions.text: In SN2 reactions, the hybridization of carbon in moving from substart to transition state changes from:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: In SN2 reactions, sp2 to sp3 hybridization takes place when the carbon atom moves from transition state to final product. It is contrary to the given statement. Hence, this option is incorrect.
In SN2 reactions, we use primary alkyl halides which are not sp hybridized. Hence, this option is incorrect, because sp to sp2 hybridization cannot take place.

In SN2 reactions, sp3 to sp2 hybridization takes place. The alkyl halide has a carbon atom that is sp3 hybridized. During the transition state, it changes to sp2. Hence, this option is the correct option.
In SN2 reactions, the alkyl halide has a carbon atom that is sp3 hybridized. Hence, sp to sp3 hybridization cannot take place. So, this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Normally, a carbon atom is sp3 hybridized. In the transition state of the SN2 mechanism, the carbon atom is sp2 hybridized due to its planar structure. At this point, carbon almost acquires 'pentavalency' with three full bonds and two 'partial' bonds, and a planar complex is formed, which is sp2 hybridized. Hence, option C is correct.

questions.text: I- is an example of _ ?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: An electrophile is an electron deficient species and often has a positive change. I- does not have a positive charge on it, so this option is incorrect.
A nucleophile is a chemical species that has either a negative charge or a lone pair. I- has negative charge, hence, it is a nucleophile and the statement is true.

A leaving group refers to that atom in SN reactions which leaves the reactant compound. I- may act as a leaving group in SN reactions. So, this statement is also true. As I- acts both as a nucleophile and a leaving group, this option is the correct option.
As I- acts both as a nucleophile and a leaving group, this option is the correct option.

questions.explanation: A nucleophile is a chemical species that has either a negative charge or a lone pair. I- has negative charge, hence, it is a nucleophile and the statement is true. A leaving group refers to that atom in SN reactions which leaves the reactant compound.I- may act as a leaving group in SN reactions. So, this statement is also true. As I- acts both as a nucleophile and a leaving group, D option is the correct option.

questions.text: High molecular mass organic compounds, upon hydrolysis yield amino acids are called _.
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This option is incorrect because On hydrolysis, these carbohydrates yield a large number of monosaccharides. Example: Starch, glycogen, and cellulose.
This option is incorrect because Hydrolysis can break down a fat or oil and release the triglyceride and fatty acids.

Proteins can be classified as: (a) Simple proteins. On hydrolysis they yield only the amino acids and occasional small carbohydrate compounds. Examples are: albumins, globulins, glutelins, albuminoids, histones and protamines Proteins are organic compounds with a high molecular weight formed of carbon, oxygen, hydrogen and nitrogen and may also contain sulfur, phosphorus and non-protein organic groups and metal ions. They are polymers formed of subunits called amino acids linked together by peptide linkage.
This option is incorrect because the complete hydrolysis of DNA yields β-D-2-deoxyribose, phosphoric acid, and adenine.

questions.explanation: Correct option is C. Proteins can be classified as: (a) Simple proteins. On hydrolysis they yield only the amino acids and occasional small carbohydrate compounds. Examples are: albumins, globulins, glutelins, albuminoids, histones and protamines Proteins are organic compounds with a high molecular weight formed of carbon, oxygen, hydrogen and nitrogen and may also contain sulfur, phosphorus and non-protein organic groups and metal ions. They are polymers formed of subunits called amino acids linked together by peptide linkage.

questions.text: Which one of the following is not a organic compound?

options.explanation: Urea is a organic compound. So , this is incorrect.
Methane is a organic compound. So , this is incorrect.
Organic compounds of carbon are classified as organic when carbon is bound to hydrogen. Organic molecules must contain carbon atoms covalently bonded to hydrogen atoms.
Coal is a organic compound. So , this is incorrect.

questions.explanation: Organic compounds of carbon are classified as organic when carbon is bound to hydrogen. Organic molecules must contain carbon atoms covalently bonded to hydrogen atoms.

questions.text: According to vital force theory organic compounds were only produced in _?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: According to the vital force theory, animals were considered to possess a vital force or vital principle that enabled them to produce organic compounds. This theory suggested that organic substances found in animals, such as proteins, fats, and carbohydrates, were not possible to be synthesized artificially or outside of living organisms.

Similarly, the vital force theory proposed that plants also had a vital force that allowed them to produce organic compounds. This theory attributed the synthesis of organic substances found in plants, including sugars, cellulose, and various secondary metabolites, to their vital force.

The vital force theory extended its concept to bacteria as well. It suggested that bacteria possessed a vital force that facilitated the production of organic compounds within their cells. This theory considered bacteria as living organisms capable of synthesizing various organic substances required for their growth and survival.

The vital force theory, organic compounds were believed to be exclusively produced in all living organisms, including animals, plants, and bacteria. The theory proposed that the synthesis of organic substances was a characteristic feature of living organisms and could not be replicated artificially outside of them.

questions.explanation: Option A: According to the vital force theory, animals were considered to possess a vital force or vital principle that enabled them to produce organic compounds. This theory suggested that organic substances found in animals, such as proteins, fats, and carbohydrates, were not possible to be synthesized artificially or outside of living organisms. Option B: Similarly, the vital force theory proposed that plants also had a vital force that allowed them to produce organic compounds. This theory attributed the synthesis of organic substances found in plants, including sugars, cellulose, and various secondary metabolites, to their vital force. Option C: The vital force theory extended its concept to bacteria as well. It suggested that bacteria possessed a vital force that facilitated the production of organic compounds within their cells. This theory considered bacteria as living organisms capable of synthesizing various organic substances required for their growth and survival. Option D: The vital force theory, organic compounds were believed to be exclusively produced in all living organisms, including animals, plants, and bacteria. The theory proposed that the synthesis of organic substances was a characteristic feature of living organisms and could not be replicated artificially outside of them.

questions.text: Dalton's atomic theory couldn't explain the concept about _?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Isomers are compounds that have the same molecular formula but different structural arrangements or spatial orientations. Dalton's atomic theory could not explain the concept of isomers because it focused on the idea that atoms were indivisible and did not consider the possibility of different structural arrangements within molecules. But according to PTB, Dalton only could not explain concept of isotopes.

Protons are positively charged subatomic particles found in the nucleus of an atom. Dalton's atomic theory did not account for the existence of protons. It proposed that atoms were indivisible and did not delve into the internal structure of atoms or their subatomic particles.But according to PTB, Dalton only could not explain concept of isotopes.

Isotopes are atoms of the same element that have different numbers of neutrons, resulting in different atomic masses. Dalton's atomic theory did not explain the concept of isotopes since it assumed that all atoms of a given element had the same mass. The discovery of isotopes came later through advancements in technology and scientific research.But according to PTB, Dalton only could not explain concept of isotopes.

The nucleus is the central part of an atom that contains protons and neutrons. Dalton's atomic theory did not account for the presence of a nucleus. It proposed that atoms were indivisible and did not address the existence or structure of a central nucleus within the atom. The discovery of the atomic nucleus came later with the experiments of scientists such as Ernest Rutherford.But according to PTB, Dalton only could not explain concept of isotopes.

questions.explanation: Option A: Isomers are compounds that have the same molecular formula but different structural arrangements or spatial orientations. Dalton's atomic theory could not explain the concept of isomers because it focused on the idea that atoms were indivisible and did not consider the possibility of different structural arrangements within molecules.Option B: Protons are positively charged subatomic particles found in the nucleus of an atom. Dalton's atomic theory did not account for the existence of protons. It proposed that atoms were indivisible and did not delve into the internal structure of atoms or their subatomic particles.Option C: Isotopes are atoms of the same element that have different numbers of neutrons, resulting in different atomic masses. Dalton's atomic theory did not explain the concept of isotopes since it assumed that all atoms of a given element had the same mass. The discovery of isotopes came later through advancements in technology and scientific research.Option D: The nucleus is the central part of an atom that contains protons and neutrons. Dalton's atomic theory did not account for the presence of a nucleus. It proposed that atoms were indivisible and did not address the existence or structure of a central nucleus within the atom. The discovery of the atomic nucleus came later with the experiments of scientists such as Ernest Rutherford.But according to PTB, Dalton could only not explain the concept of isotopes. He had no link with isomers, protons and nucleus.

questions.text: Atomicity is determined by the number of _ present in a molecule.
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This option is not directly related to atomicity. Dots are often used in structural formulas or Lewis dot diagrams to represent the valence electrons of atoms, but they do not determine the atomicity of a molecule.

Atomicity refers to the number of atoms bonded together to form a molecule. The number of atoms present in a molecule determines its atomicity. For example, a molecule of oxygen gas (O2) has an atomicity of 2 because it consists of two oxygen atoms bonded together.

Sub particles generally refer to particles that make up atoms, such as protons, neutrons, and electrons. While these sub particles contribute to the overall structure of atoms, they do not directly determine the atomicity of a molecule.

Electrons are negatively charged subatomic particles that orbit around the nucleus of an atom. While electrons play a crucial role in chemical bonding, they alone do not determine the atomicity of a molecule. The atomicity is determined by the number of atoms present in a molecule.

questions.explanation: Option A: This option is not directly related to atomicity. Dots are often used in structural formulas or Lewis dot diagrams to represent the valence electrons of atoms, but they do not determine the atomicity of a molecule. For example, a molecule of oxygen gas (O2) has an atomicity of 2 because it consists of two oxygen atoms bonded together. While these sub particles contribute to the overall structure of atoms, they do not directly determine the atomicity of a molecule. Option D: Electrons are negatively charged subatomic particles that orbit around the nucleus of an atom. While electrons play a crucial role in chemical bonding, they alone do not determine the atomicity of a molecule. The atomicity is determined by the number of atoms present in a molecule.

questions.text: The separation of components of liquid on the basis of their boiling points is called as?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Destructive distillation is a process that involves heating organic material in the absence of air or oxygen to break it down into simpler chemical compounds.

Fractional distillation is a process used to separate a mixture of two or more liquids with different boiling points. This process relies on the fact that each liquid in the mixture has its own unique boiling point.

Vacuum distillation is a type of distillation used to separate materials with high boiling points or those that are sensitive to heat. In vacuum distillation, the pressure inside the distillation apparatus is lowered, which reduces the boiling point of the material being distilled.
There is no such term as partial distillation.

questions.explanation: Destructive distillation is a process that involves heating organic material in the absence of air or oxygen to break it down into simpler chemical compounds. Fractional distillation is a process used to separate a mixture of two or more liquids with different boiling points. This process relies on the fact that each liquid in the mixture has its own unique boiling point. Vacuum distillation is a type of distillation used to separate materials with high boiling points or those that are sensitive to heat. In vacuum distillation, the pressure inside the distillation apparatus is lowered, which reduces the boiling point of the material being distilled. There is no such term as partial distillation.

questions.text: Large hydrocarbons are converted into smaller hydrocarbons by a process called as?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Reforming is a process in which hydrocarbons are converted into more branched or cyclic hydrocarbons by rearranging the carbon-carbon bonds.
Distillation is a process of separating a mixture of liquids based on their boiling points. It is not used for the conversion of large hydrocarbons into smaller hydrocarbons.

Cracking is a process in which large hydrocarbon molecules are broken down into smaller, more useful molecules by breaking carbon-carbon bonds. This process is typically carried out by heating the hydrocarbon to high temperatures (usually between 400-900°C) in the presence of a catalyst or steam.

Decomposition is a general term used to describe the breakdown of a substance into simpler compounds or elements, often by heat or chemical reactions. It is not specific to the conversion of hydrocarbons into smaller hydrocarbons

questions.explanation: Reforming is a process in which hydrocarbons are converted into more branched or cyclic hydrocarbons by rearranging the carbon-carbon bonds. Distillation is a process of separating a mixture of liquids based on their boiling points. It is not used for the conversion of large hydrocarbons into smaller hydrocarbons. Cracking is a process in which large hydrocarbon molecules are broken down into smaller, more useful molecules by breaking carbon-carbon bonds. This process is typically carried out by heating the hydrocarbon to high temperatures (usually between 400-900°C) in the presence of a catalyst or steam. Decomposition is a general term used to describe the breakdown of a substance into simpler compounds or elements, often by heat or chemical reactions. It is not specific to the conversion of hydrocarbons into smaller hydrocarbons

questions.text: Organic compounds are _..
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: a) Ionic: Ionic compounds are formed through the transfer of electrons between atoms, resulting in the formation of positively charged ions (cations) and negatively charged ions (anions). Organic compounds, on the other hand, are primarily composed of carbon and hydrogen atoms and are typically covalent in nature, not ionic.

b) Non-ionic: Non-ionic compounds do not contain ions. Instead, they consist of molecules held together by non-ionic interactions, such as hydrogen bonding or van der Waals forces. Organic compounds can indeed be non-ionic, as they often form covalent bonds between atoms.

c) Non-covalent: Non-covalent interactions are relatively weak forces of attraction between molecules, including hydrogen bonding, van der Waals forces, and electrostatic interactions. While some organic compounds can exhibit non-covalent interactions, it is not a defining characteristic of all organic compounds.

d) Covalent: Covalent bonds involve the sharing of electrons between atoms to form molecules. Organic compounds are indeed covalent, as they are composed of carbon and hydrogen atoms primarily held together by covalent bonds.

questions.explanation: By the definition of covalent bonds, it is formed due to sharing one / more pair of electrons between two atoms. In organic compounds the bonds between two carbon atoms is due to sharing of electrons, hence it is covalent. So, bonds in organic compounds are co valent. Option A,B and C are incorrect.

questions.text: Which types of bonds are broken during cracking?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: During cracking, larger hydrocarbon molecules are broken down into smaller molecules through a process of thermal decomposition. This process involves breaking the carbon-carbon (C-C) bonds present in the hydrocarbon molecules. Cracking is commonly used in the petroleum industry to produce valuable products like gasoline and other fuels.

C-H bonds are not typically broken during cracking. These bonds are relatively strong and stable, and the primary purpose of cracking is to break down larger hydrocarbon molecules by breaking C-C bonds to obtain smaller, more useful molecules.

While C-C bonds are indeed broken during cracking, C-H bonds are generally not the focus of this process. Cracking mainly involves breaking C-C bonds to initiate the breakdown of larger hydrocarbons into smaller ones.

C-O bonds are not commonly broken during traditional thermal cracking processes. However, in some specific cases, such as steam cracking, certain oxygen-containing compounds might be present, and C-O bonds could potentially be broken as part of the cracking process.

questions.explanation: The primary types of bonds that are broken during cracking are C-C bonds. Cracking involves the thermal decomposition of larger hydrocarbon molecules by breaking the carbon-carbon bonds, leading to the formation of smaller, more useful molecules. C-H bonds are generally not broken during the cracking process.Taken from a bookline in PTB

questions.text: Gasoline produced by the Fractional distillation is _?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: 80%: Gasoline produced by fractional distillation does not typically account for 80% of the total yield. The percentage of gasoline obtained through fractional distillation is much lower than 80%.

5%: Gasoline produced by fractional distillation is not usually as low as 5% of the total yield. Gasoline is a valuable and widely used product, so it generally represents a larger portion of the refined products.

65%: Gasoline produced by fractional distillation is not commonly around 65% of the total yield. While the percentage of gasoline can vary depending on the specific crude oil and refining process, it is typically lower than 65%.
It's a fact that fractional distillation produces 20% gasoline

questions.explanation: (It's a fact that fractional distillation produces 20% gasoline)

questions.text: Which catalysts are used in catalytic cracking?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: A is incorrect since a mixture of silica and lime is used to make cement.
B is correct since silica and alumina are used in catalytic cracking to produce smaller and more useful fractions from larger hydrocarbons.
C is incorrect since silica and soda ash are used in the process of glass manufacturing.
D is incorrect since alumina and platinum are used in the process of cyclohexane dehydrogenation.

questions.explanation: Option B is correct since silica and alumina are used in catalytic cracking to produce smaller more useful fractions from larger hydrocarbons.Option A is incorrect since a mixture of silica and lime is used to make cement.Option C is incorrect since silica and soda ash is used in the process of glass manufacturing.Option D is incorrect since alumina and platinum are used in the process of cyclohexane dehydrogenation.

questions.text: Liquid hydrocarbons is converted into gaseous hydrocarbon by:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Cracking is the process in which long chain hydrocarbons (in liquid/solid state) are broken into short chain, more useful hydrocarbons which have a smaller molecular mass/no. of atoms resulting in weaker instantaneous dipole induced dipole forces to hold the molecules close together so they are in gaseous form. Hence, there is a state change.Hydrolysis and oxidation have nothing to do with state change. The final product of distillation is collected in liquid form.

False.Cracking is the process in which long chain hydrocarbons (in liquid/solid state) are broken into short chain, more useful hydrocarbons which have a smaller molecular mass/no. of atoms resulting in weaker instantaneous dipole induced dipole forces to hold the molecules close together so they are in gaseous form. Hence, there is a state change.Hydrolysis and oxidation have nothing to do with state change. The final product of distillation is collected in liquid form.
False. Oxidation is addition of oxygen.
This is not true as per the Explanation.

questions.explanation: Cracking is the process in which long chain hydrocarbons (in liquid/solid state) are broken into short chain, more useful hydrocarbons which have a smaller molecular mass/no. of atoms resulting in weaker instantaneous dipole induced dipole forces to hold the molecules close together so they are in gaseous form. Hence, there is a state change. Hydrolysis and oxidation have nothing to do with state change. The final product of distillation is collected in liquid form.

questions.text: Organic compounds are obtained from:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Many organic compounds are obtained from living organisms, such as carbohydrates, proteins, and lipids. These compounds are produced naturally by living organisms as part of their metabolic processes and are essential for life. Other organic compounds, such as pharmaceuticals and natural products, can also be obtained from living organisms, such as plants and bacteria, through the process of extraction and purification.

Organic compounds are not obtained from non-living sources such as fossil fuels. Fossil fuels are primarily composed of hydrocarbons, which are organic compounds, but they are not obtained directly from living organisms. Instead, they are formed from the remains of ancient plants and animals that have undergone chemical transformations over millions of years. Organic compounds can also be synthesized in the laboratory using a variety of chemical reactions and techniques.

This option is incorrect. There are concerns about the environmental impact of extracting and burning fossil fuels, as they release carbon dioxide and other pollutants into the atmosphere, contributing to climate change and air pollution. As a result, there is growing interest in developing alternative sources of energy and materials that are more sustainable and less harmful to the environment.

While seawater does contain a variety of dissolved organic compounds, these compounds are typically present in very low concentrations and are not typically obtained from seawater as a source. Instead, organic compounds are more commonly obtained from living organisms, such as plants and animals, or from non-living sources such as fossil fuels or through chemical synthesis in the laboratory.

questions.explanation: Organic compounds are obtained from living things. Among the numerous types of organic compounds, four major categories are found in all "living things": carbohydrates, lipids, proteins, and nucleic acids. Many organic compounds are formed from chains of covalently-linked carbon atoms with hydrogen atoms attached to chains. The main sources of organic compounds are plant and animal kingdoms.

questions.text: Aromatic compounds generally burn with smoky flame because:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: They have ring structure of carbon atoms.
options.explanation: The aromatic compound displays aromaticity. They burn with a strong sooty yellow flame. A "sooty flame" indicates that incomplete combustion is occurring and the reason for this flame is the high percentage of carbon content. The carbon-hydrogen ratio is high in aromatic compound.

options.text: They have a relatively high percentage of hydrogen atoms.

questions.text: Coal, natural gas and petroleum are generally called:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: a) Fossil fuels: This is the correct classification. Coal, natural gas, and petroleum are all fossil fuels. Fossil fuels are hydrocarbons that formed from the remains of plants and animals that lived millions of years ago. Over time, these organic materials were subjected to heat and pressure, transforming them into coal, natural gas, and petroleum. They are considered non-renewable resources because their formation takes millions of years, and their extraction and consumption deplete their reserves over time.

b) Renewable sources: Renewable sources refer to energy sources that are naturally replenished and can be used indefinitely. Examples of renewable sources include solar energy, wind energy, hydroelectric power, and geothermal energy. Unlike fossil fuels, these sources are not derived from ancient organic matter and can be harnessed without depleting their resources.

c) Infinite sources: "Infinite sources" is not a standard term used in the context of energy or natural resources. The term "infinite" implies that the sources are endless and can never be exhausted, which is not applicable to any finite resource, including coal, natural gas, or petroleum.
d) None of the above: As explained above, the correct classification for coal, natural gas, and petroleum is "fossil fuels." Therefore, option d) "None of the above" is not the correct answer.

questions.explanation: Coal, natural gas and petroleum are all generally called fossil fuels and are nonrenewable sources of energy.

questions.text: Which type of organic compounds are present in natural gas?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This option is incorrect because natural gas primarily consists of low molecular mass compounds, not high molecular mass organic compounds, which are typically found in petroleum.
This option is partially correct as natural gas contains low molecular mass hydrocarbons, contributing to its properties as a fuel.
This option is also partially correct because natural gas is made up of low boiling point hydrocarbons, which are essential for its gaseous state at room temperature.
This is the correct answer. Natural gas is a mixture of hydrocarbons that have both low molecular mass and low boiling point characteristics, primarily consisting of methane.

questions.explanation: Natural gas is primarily composed of methane (CH₂), along with smaller amounts of other light alkanes like ethane and propane. These are all low molecular mass hydrocarbons, which consequently have very low boiling points.

questions.text: Which of the following is correct about octane number?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.text: Higher the octane number, higher will be knocking
options.explanation: The octane number is a measure of a fuel's resistance to knocking. So, the higher the octane number the higher the knocking is not correct.

options.text: Higher the octane number, efficiency of fuel will be lower
options.explanation: The octane number is simply a measure of the fuel's ability to resist knocking, not its energy content or efficiency in terms of power production.

options.text: Higher the octane number, lower the knocking
options.explanation: The octane number is a measure of a fuel's resistance to knocking. As the octane number increases, the engine is less likely to produce knocking.

options.text: Lower the octane number, lower the knocking
options.explanation: The octane number is a measure of a fuel's resistance to knocking. So, the lower the octane number the higher the knocking will be.

questions.explanation: The octane number is a measure of a fuel's resistance to knocking. As the octane number increases, the engine is less likely to produce knocking.

questions.text: Which of the following has antiknock properties?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Tetra-methyl lead contains lead, which is an efficient antiknock agent, improving the quality of poor fuels when mixed with it.
Tetra-ethyl lead is another substance containing lead, which acts as an antiknock agent when mixed with poor fuels.
Isooctane has exceptional antiknock properties as it burns smoothly in engines and is assigned an octane number of 100.
Lead-based substances like tetra-methyl lead and tetra-ethyl lead, along with isooctane, possess antiknock properties, making all options correct.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'All of these' because tetra-methyl lead, tetra-ethyl lead, and isooctane all exhibit antiknock properties. Tetra-methyl lead and tetra-ethyl lead contain lead, an efficient antiknock agent, while isooctane burns smoothly with an octane number of 100. The other options are incorrect as they do not encompass all substances with antiknock properties.

questions.text: Which of the following process is used to improve the quality of gasoline?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Reforming is used to improve the quality of gasoline because it converts low-octane naphtha into high-octane reformates. Octane rating is a measure of a fuel's ability to resist knocking, which is an uncontrolled combustion that can damage an engine. High-octane gasoline is less likely to knock than low-octane gasoline, so it is better for performance and fuel economy.

questions.text: Methane is obtained by which following method:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Electrolysis of sodium acetate does not produce methane. Instead, it produces ethane due to the formation of methyl radicals that combine to form ethane. This method is not suitable for producing methane.
This process does not produce methane. The electrolysis of aluminium carbide is not typically used to produce hydrocarbons. Instead, it involves reactions that consume carbon electrodes.

options.text: Heating sodium acetate with soda lime
options.explanation: This is the correct method. When sodium acetate (CH3COONa) is heated with soda lime (a mixture of NaOH and CaO), it undergoes a decarboxylation reaction that produces methane (CH4) and sodium carbonate.
This option is incorrect because only heating sodium acetate with soda lime produces methane. The other methods do not produce methane.

questions.explanation: The correct method for obtaining methane is by heating sodium acetate with soda lime. This process involves a decarboxylation reaction where sodium acetate reacts with soda lime to produce methane (CH4) and sodium carbonate. The other options are incorrect: Electrolysis of sodium acetate produces ethane, not methane, due to the formation of methyl radicals. Electrolysis of aluminium carbide is not used for methane production, as it involves different reactions consuming carbon electrodes.

questions.text: All of the following compounds are organic except:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: An organic compound is any of a large class of chemical compounds in which one or more atoms of carbon are covalently linked to atoms of other elements, most commonly hydrogen, oxygen, or nitrogen. The few carbon-containing compounds not classified as organic include carbides, carbonates, and cyanides. The study of the properties, reactions, and syntheses of organic compounds comprise the discipline known as organic chemistry.Examples of organic compounds are carbohydrates, fats (lipids), proteins, and nucleic acids, which are the basis for the molecules of life. Organic compounds also include petroleum and natural gas, which are the main components of fossil fuels.Inorganic compounds do not contain any C-H bond or functional group directly attached to carbon, for example, KOCN, HCN, iodine, etc.
Option B has a direct C-H bond making it organic.
Option C has a direct C-H bond making it organic.
Option D has a direct C-H bond making it organic.

questions.explanation: Organic compound is any of a large class of chemical compounds in which one or more atoms of carbon are covalently linked to atoms of other elements, most commonly hydrogen, oxygen, or nitrogen. The few carbon-containing compounds not classified as organic include carbides, carbonates, and cyanides. The study of the properties, reactions, and syntheses of organic compounds comprise the discipline known as organic chemistry.Examples of organic compounds are carbohydrates, fats (lipids), proteins, and nucleic acids, which are the basis for the molecules of life. Organic compounds also include petroleum and natural gas, which are the main components of fossil fuels.Inorganic compounds do not contain any C-H bond or functional group directly attached to carbon for example, KOCN, HCN and iodine etc.Options B,C and D have direct C-H bond making them organic.

questions.text: Catenation is a process in which carbon shows the properties of making:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Multiple bonds are formed by a process called elimination.
Hybridization is just a process that helps in bonding ( can be single, double, or triple).

options.text: Long chain or rings of carbon atoms
options.explanation: Catenation of carbon.
Isomerism is a process of compounds having the same molecular formula but different structural formulas.

questions.explanation: Catenation is the bonding of atoms of the same element into a series, called a chain. A chain or a ring shape may be open if its ends are not bonded to each other (an open-chain compound), or closed if they are bonded in a ring (a cyclic compound).

questions.text: Which one of the following compounds has a sharp melting point?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Pure substances generally have sharp melting points. When a pure compound like glucose is heated, it undergoes a phase change from solid to liquid at a specific temperature, resulting in a sharp melting point.

This option is not correct. Impure substances, such as impure NaCl, tend to have a broad melting point. This is because impurities in the substance can interfere with the regular arrangement of particles, causing a range of temperatures at which melting occurs.
Glass is not a compound but an amorphous solid. Amorphous solids do not have a sharp melting point.

questions.explanation: Pure substances generally have sharp melting points. When a pure compound like glucose is heated, it undergoes a phase change from solid to liquid at a specific temperature, resulting in a sharp melting point.

questions.text: A nucleophile is
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Lewis acid: A Lewis acid is an electron pair acceptor. It has an empty orbital that can accept a pair of electrons. This is the opposite of what a nucleophile does.

Bronsted acid: A Brønsted acid is a proton (H^+) donor. While some nucleophiles can also act as Brønsted bases (proton acceptors), the defining characteristic of a nucleophile is its ability to donate an electron pair, not necessarily accept a proton.

Bronsted base: A Brønsted base is a proton (H^+) acceptor. While many nucleophiles are also Brønsted bases because they possess lone pairs of electrons that can accept a proton, the fundamental definition of a nucleophile revolves around electron pair donation.

Nucleophile: The term "nucleophile" means "nucleus-loving". Nucleophiles are electron-rich species that are attracted to positive charges or electron-deficient centers (nuclei). They donate a pair of electrons to form a new covalent bond.

questions.explanation: A nucleophile is a chemical species that donates an electron pair to form a new chemical bond. It is characterized by its electron-rich nature and its ability to participate in nucleophilic reactions. In Lewis acid-base theory, a nucleophile is considered a Lewis base, as it can donate electrons to form a coordinate covalent bond.

questions.text: All compounds are organic except
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Urea is classified as an organic compound because it contains a carbon atom that is bonded to nitrogen and hydrogen atoms, fulfilling the criteria for organic compounds.
Ammonium cyanate does not have any C-H covalent bonds, which are essential for defining organic compounds. Therefore, it is categorized as an inorganic compound.
This compound contains a carbon atom that is bonded to hydrogen atoms, thus qualifying it as an organic compound due to the presence of C-H bonds.
Methane is a quintessential organic compound as it consists solely of carbon and hydrogen atoms bonded together, exemplifying the key characteristics of organic compounds.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is ammonium cyanate (NH4CNO), which is not considered an organic compound as it lacks carbon-hydrogen (C-H) bonds, a fundamental characteristic of organic chemistry. In contrast, urea (H₂N)₂CO, CH₂NO₂, and methane (CH4) all contain C-H bonds, thus categorizing them as organic compounds. The absence of C-H bonds in ammonium cyanate distinguishes it as inorganic.

questions.text: The first organic compound prepared in the laboratory was:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. The first organic compound prepared in the laboratory was urea.

The first organic compound prepared in the laboratory was urea. Urea was synthesized by Friedrich Wöhler in 1828 from inorganic starting materials (ammonium chloride and silver cyanate). This experiment is considered a landmark in the history of chemistry because it showed that organic compounds, which were believed to be produced only by living organisms, could also be synthesized from inorganic substances.
This option is incorrect. The first organic compound prepared in the laboratory was urea.
This option is incorrect. The first organic compound prepared in the laboratory was urea.

questions.explanation: The first organic compound prepared in the laboratory was urea. Urea was synthesized by Friedrich Wöhler in 1828 from inorganic starting materials (ammonium chloride and silver cyanate). This experiment is considered a landmark in the history of chemistry because it showed that organic compounds, which were believed to be produced only by living organisms, could also be synthesized from inorganic substances. Therefore, option b is correct.

questions.text: Nail polish removers are mainly composed of:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Propyne is a different compound, an alkyne, and is not commonly used in nail polish removers.

Nail polish removers are mainly composed of propanone, which is more commonly known as acetone. Acetone is a powerful solvent that is effective in removing nail polish. It works by dissolving the nail polish, making it easier to wipe or wash away.
Propanal, also known as propionaldehyde, is an aldehyde and is not a common component of nail polish removers.
Propene is a different compound, an alkene, and is not typically used in nail polish removers.

questions.explanation: Nail polish removers are mainly composed of propanone, which is more commonly known as acetone. Acetone is a powerful solvent that is effective in removing nail polish. It works by dissolving the nail polish, making it easier to wipe or wash away. Therefore, option b is correct.

questions.text: Coke is produced from bituminous coal by
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Cracking is a process mainly associated with breaking down large hydrocarbon molecules into smaller ones, typically in the oil refining industry. It is not used for coke production from coal.
Synthesis refers to combining simpler substances to form a complex substance, which is not the process for producing coke from coal.
Substitution involves replacing one element or group in a compound with another, which is unrelated to the production of coke from coal.
Destructive distillation is the correct process for producing coke from bituminous coal. It involves heating coal in the absence of air, breaking it down into coke and other byproducts.

questions.explanation: Destructive distillation is the correct process used to produce coke from bituminous coal. In this process, coal is heated in the absence of air, leading to its breakdown into coke, volatile gases, and other byproducts. Coke is an important fuel and a source of carbon, particularly in the iron and steel industries.The other options are incorrect: Cracking is related to breaking down hydrocarbons in petroleum refining, not coal. Synthesis refers to creating complex compounds from simpler ones, which does not apply here. Substitution involves one chemical replacing another in a compound, which is unrelated to coke production.

questions.text: Which of the following is not an organic compound
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: HCN (Hydrogen Cyanide) is often debated in classification but is not considered a classic organic compound due to the absence of C-H bonds typical in organic chemistry.

(Methylmagnesium Iodide) is an organometallic compound, noted for its carbon-metal (C-Mg) bond. While it contains carbon, it falls under organometallic chemistry, distinct from conventional organic compounds.
(Carbon Tetrachloride) is classified as an organic compound. It comprises carbon bonded to non-metal atoms like chlorine, fitting the typical definition of organic compounds.
(Chloroform) is an organic compound as it contains carbon bonded to hydrogen and other non-metal atoms, falling squarely within the realm of organic chemistry.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is CH3Mgl (Methylmagnesium Iodide), which is an organometallic compound characterized by a carbon-metal bond. While it contains carbon, it is distinct from traditional organic compounds, which are defined by carbon bonded to hydrogen or other non-metal atoms.HCN is not typically considered a classic organic compound, but it is often debated due to its unique bonding configurations.CCl4 and CHCl3 both fit the standard definition of organic compounds as they involve carbon bonded to non-metal atoms like hydrogen and chlorine.

questions.text: The process of fermentation involves all the enzymes except
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Propane is not related to the fermentation process.
Propanal is not typically involved in fermentation.
Propanoic acid is not typically involved in fermentation.
Propanol 1 (also known as 1-propanol or n-propanol) can be produced during fermentation, making it a relevant component.

questions.explanation: The process of fermentation typically involves enzymes that convert sugars into alcohol and carbon dioxide. Among the options provided: A) Propane is not related to the fermentation process. B) Propanal is not typically involved in fermentation. C) Propanoic acid is not typically involved in fermentation. D) Propanol 1 (also known as 1-propanol or n-propanol) can be produced during fermentation, making it a relevant component. So, the answer is B) Propanal.

questions.text: The initial discovery of natural gas in Pakistan dates back to _ when it was found in the Sui area of Baluchistan:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. Although the 1950s were significant for energy discoveries in Pakistan, the actual year of discovery was earlier.

questions.explanation: The discovery of natural gas in the Sui area of Balochistan in 1952 was a landmark event in Pakistan's history, initiating the development of its natural energy resources. This discovery was crucial for the country's energy infrastructure. Options 1955, 1954, and 1956 are incorrect because they do not align with the actual year of discovery, and 1992 is far too late, occurring decades after the initial event.

questions.text: Urea was first synthesized by Wohler from an inorganic material named:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. Ammonium bicarbonate was not used by Wöhler for the synthesis of urea.
This option is incorrect. Ammonium nitrate is not related to the synthesis of urea by Wöhler.
This option is incorrect. Ammonium oxalate was not the inorganic material used by Wöhler to synthesize urea.

This option is correct. Urea was first synthesized by Wöhler from an inorganic material named ammonium cyanate. This groundbreaking synthesis in 1828 marked the first time an organic compound was artificially synthesized from inorganic substances, challenging the idea of 'vitalism' in chemistry.
This option is incorrect. Ammonium chloride was not the compound Wöhler used to synthesize urea.

questions.explanation: Urea was synthesized by Friedrich Wöhler in 1828 using ammonium cyanate. This synthesis was significant because it demonstrated that organic compounds could be created from inorganic materials, which contradicted the prevailing belief in 'vitalism' — the idea that organic compounds could only be produced by living organisms. The other options, such as ammonium bicarbonate, ammonium nitrate, ammonium oxalate, and ammonium chloride, were not involved in Wöhler's synthesis of urea.
